stemHtml: Due to high arousal or overstress, an individual starts to hyperventilate. Consequently, the carbon dioxide level in the blood is lowered which results in:

options.a: A poor saturation of oxygen in the blood.
options.b: Less oxygen to be diffused into the cells.
options.c: A delay in the onset of hypoxia when flying at high altitudes.
options.d: The activation of the respiratory centre, which in turn causes hypoxia.
